There is no information on the plant Feuilleea odoratissima. However, I can provide information on a plant called Fevillea odoratissima. 

Fevillea odoratissima is a plant species, commonly known as the "fragrant creeper" or "odoratissima", native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. 

The health benefits of this plant are not well-documented, but it is believed to have potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. 

The bioactive constituents of Fevillea odoratissima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, although further research is needed to confirm their exact composition. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Fevillea odoratissima are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
